chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. Cr. Bail Appl. No.2598 of 2024 (Muhammad Asim Imran vs. The State) Cr. Bail Appl. No.2315 of 2024 (Osama vs. The State) For hearing of Bail Application. 10.12.2024. Mr. Arshad Riaz Mughal, advocate for applicant in Cr. B.A. No.2598/2024 Mr. Asghar Bangash, advocate for applicant in Cr. B.A. No.2315/2024 Mr. Saleem Akhtar, Addl: PG Sindh O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Four unidentified persons committed robbery from the Petrol Pump at Quaid-e-Azam Square Malir, Karachi of complainant from his cashier, namely, Zulfiqar on 29.07.2024, the FIR of which was registered on 04.08.2024. During investigation, applicants were arrested in different crimes and were put to identification parade in which both the applicants have been picked up to be the robbers. 2. Learned counsel for the applicants have submitted that the identification parade has not been properly held. There is no name in the FIR. No role has been assigned to the applicants, hence, they are entitled to bail. 3. On the other hand, learned Addl: PG Sindh has opposed the bail on the ground that since applicants have been picked up by the witnesses, hence, no case for bail is made out. 4. I have heard submissions and agree with learned Addl: PG Sindh that in this case the applicants have been picked up by the witnesses, who have no motive to falsely implicate them in this heinous offence. Any lacuna, irregularity in identification parade can only be appreciated after recording of evidence as it requires deeper appreciations of the evidence. It is stated that charge has been framed and the trial is about to commence. No case for bail is made out.

It is stated that charge has been framed and the trial is about to commence. No case for bail is made out. Accordingly, these bail applications are dismissed. 5. Bail applications stand disposed of in the above terms. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. Office to place a copy of this order in connected matter. J U D G E Rafiq/P.A
